data.title: India's major prop firms for 2025, rated by PropFirms
data.description:
The best prop firms for Indian traders offer low-cost challenges, high profit splits, fast payouts, and access to key markets like USD/INR and FTSE 100 Index. PropFirms tested trading conditions, challenge difficulty, and profit-sharing models to find the best prop firms in India for 2025.

  1. FundedNext - Scaling Plan to $4 million
  2. FXIFY - Best Prop Firm in India
  3. DNA Funded - Top Broker Backed Prop Firm
  4. BrightFunded - Best Prop Trader Rewards Program
  5. ThinkCapital - Low Fee Prop Challenges
  6. IC Funded - Top Forex Prop Firm
  7. Blueberry Funded - Good Crypto Prop Firm
  8. FTMO - Best Stock Prop Firm
  9. OANDA Prop Trader - Prop Firm for Beginners
  10. The Funded Trader - Large Prop Trading Community

1. FundedNext – Best Prop Firm in India


FundedNext is the best proprietary trading firm for Indian traders, offering flexible challenges, high-profit splits, and a scaling plan into the millions. With access to leading trading platforms like MetaTrader 4, MetaTrader 5, cTrader, and TradingView, you get the flexibility to trade with your preferred tools.

Use code PROPFIRMS for an exclusive 120% refund on challenge fees—100% refund plus an extra 20% in cash.


Evaluation Process


FundedNext provides three challenge types, catering to different trading styles and risk tolerances:
  • Evaluation Model – A structured two-step challenge with moderate profit targets and risk limits. Offers up to a 90% profit split.
  • Express Model – Instant funding option with stricter risk management rules. You can choose between Consistency and Non-Consistency accounts, with profit splits up to 95%.
  • Stellar Model – Flexible one-step and two-step options with relaxed profit targets and no time limits, making it easier to pass.

All challenge types offer free retakes and extensions, ensuring you have multiple chances to meet the profit targets.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


Once you pass the evaluation, you receive a funded account ranging from $5,000 to $200,000. Profit splits start at 80% and can reach 95%, depending on the challenge type. 

Withdrawals are processed every 14 to 28 days, and some accounts have no minimum trading days required. The scaling plan increases your account size by 40% every four months, allowing you to grow up to $4 million in trading capital if you maintain consistent profitability.

Markets & Leverage


FundedNext provides access to a wide range of financial instruments with competitive leverage:
  • Forex – Major, minor, and exotic currency pairs, including USD/INR (up to 1:100 leverage).
  • Indices – Global indices such as NIFTY 50 and US30 (up to 1:50 leverage).
  • Commodities – Gold, silver, crude oil, and natural gas (up to 1:50 leverage).
  • Cryptocurrencies – Bitcoin, Ethereum, and other major crypto pairs (up to 1:2 leverage).

Trading Platforms


FundedNext supports multiple platforms, allowing traders to choose based on their strategy and experience level:
  • MetaTrader 4 (MT4)– A popular choice for forex traders, offering automated trading via Expert Advisors (EAs).
  • MetaTrader 5 (MT5) – Features enhanced charting tools, more timeframes, and an economic calendar.
  • cTrader – Ideal for manual traders, with advanced order types and Level II pricing.
  • TradingView – Provides intuitive charting, social trading features, and a seamless web-based experience.

Trust & Support


FundedNext has a positive TrustPilot rating, reflecting trader satisfaction with its clear rules, fast payouts, and strong support network. The prop firm is highly active in the trading community, with a large Discord server, Telegram channels, and regular engagement on social media. 

Customer support is available 24/7 via live chat and email, with additional educational content and trading guides to help you succeed and learn new trading skills.

FundedNext has gained a strong reputation in the prop trading industry, largely due to its generous scaling plan and trader-focused funding options. The best prop firm comparison site is PropFirms, with its co-founder Noam Korbl sharing with us:

"FundedNext is one of the most popular prop firms worldwide, thanks to its impressive scaling plan to $4 million, one of the most substantial growth opportunities in the industry."

Final Verdict


FundedNext is the best prop trading firm in India, thanks to its flexible challenges, high profit splits, and strong scaling plan. While strict loss limits require careful risk management, its fast payouts, diverse account types, and trusted platforms make it a top choice.

Use coupon code PROPFIRMS for an exclusive 120% refund on challenge fees—100% refund plus an extra 20% in cash.

2. FXIFY – Best Instant Funding Prop Firm


FXIFY is a top prop trading firm in India, offering flexible evaluation models and an instant funding option for traders who want immediate capital. Backed by FXPIG, a broker known for low spreads and fast execution, FXIFY provides account sizes up to $400,000 with profit splits reaching 90%.

There are no current promo codes, but check PropFirms social media platforms like X and LinkedIn for the latest discounts.

Evaluation Process


FXIFY offers multiple challenge types, allowing traders to select the best fit for their strategy and risk tolerance:
  • One Phase Challenge – A single-step challenge requiring a 10% profit target with a 6% trailing drawdown, providing a fast-track route to a funded account.
  • Two Phase Challenge – A structured two-step process with a 10% profit target in Phase 1 and 5% in Phase 2, alongside a 4% daily drawdown cap, designed to reward consistency.
  • Three Phase Challenge – A gradual funding path with 5% profit targets across three phases and a 5% daily drawdown, offering a lower-risk approach.
  • Instant Funding – Skip the evaluation process entirely and access capital immediately, with profit splits of up to 90%.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


Once you pass the challenge, FXIFY provides funded accounts ranging from $5,000 to $400,000, with a base profit split of 80%. Traders can upgrade to 90% profit splits through a paid add-on. Payouts are monthly by default, but a bi-weekly withdrawal option is available for an additional fee.

FXIFY’s scaling plan rewards consistent profitability by increasing account balances by 25% every three months, allowing traders to potentially grow their capital up to $4 million.

Markets & Leverage


FXIFY provides access to a wide range of tradable assets with industry-standard leverage:
  • Forex – Major, minor, and exotic currency pairs, including USD/INR (up to 1:30 leverage, 50:1 with add-on).
  • Indices – 13 major indices, including NIFTY 50 (up to 1:10 leverage).
  • Commodities – Gold, silver, crude oil (up to 1:10 leverage).
  • Stocks – 30 global equities (available only on MetaTrader 5, with 1:2 leverage).
  • Cryptocurrencies – Bitcoin, Ethereum, and other crypto pairs (1:2 leverage).

Trading Platforms


FXIFY supports multiple trading platforms, ensuring compatibility with different trading strategies:
  • MetaTrader 4 (MT4) – A widely used platform, ideal for forex trading and automated strategies via Expert Advisors (EAs).
  • MetaTrader 5 (MT5) – An upgraded version of MT4 with advanced order types, improved charting, and support for more asset classes.
  • DXTrade – A customizable web-based platform that integrates with TradingView for advanced charting and technical analysis.

Trust & Support


FXIFY is one of the most trusted proprietary trading firms, offering fast payouts, flexible funding options, and a strong trader support system. The prop firm is highly active in the trading community, with a large Discord server, social media presence, and dedicated support team.

Customer support is available via live chat and email, ensuring traders can get assistance whenever needed. While response times can vary, FXIFY is known for its transparent communication and commitment to trader success.

In addition to its support team, FXIFY provides an engaged trading community, where traders can exchange strategies, ask questions, and stay updated on prop firm developments.

Final Verdict


FXIFY took second spot on our list of the best prop firms in India thanks to its instant funding options, flexible challenges, and competitive profit splits. The ability to trade USD/INR, NIFTY 50, and crypto markets makes it particularly attractive for Indian traders. While customisation options like higher leverage and bi-weekly payouts come at an extra cost, FXIFY remains a leading prop firm with strong funding potential and trader support.

3. DNA Funded – Top Broker Backed Prop Firm


DNA Funded is the third best prop firm in India, offering low-cost challenges, broker-backed liquidity, and wide market access. Partnered with ASIC-regulated DNA Markets, it provides tight spreads, fast execution, and flexible account options. With no time limits on most challenges and add-ons for faster payouts and higher profit splits, it's a strong choice for traders seeking reliable funding.

Use promo code PROPFIRMS25 for an exclusive 25% discount on challenge fees.


Evaluation Process


DNA Funded provides three challenge types, each catering to different trading strategies and experience levels:
  • Single Helix Challenge – A one-step challenge with moderate profit targets and no time limits, ideal for traders who prefer a relaxed evaluation process.
  • Double Helix Challenge – A two-step challenge designed for disciplined traders, with structured profit targets and standard risk parameters.
  • Pro Challenge – A time-restricted challenge suited for high-frequency traders, featuring a lower profit target but stricter drawdown rules.

All challenges offer add-ons for faster payouts and increased profit splits, allowing traders to customise their evaluation experience.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


After passing the evaluation, you can access funded accounts ranging from $5,000 to $200,000. DNA Funded offers an 80% profit split by default, with an option to increase it to 90% via a paid add-on. Withdrawals are processed every 14 days, but traders can reduce this to 7 days by purchasing an early payout add-on. Unlike many proprietary trading firms, DNA Funded allows profit withdrawals from as little as $1, ensuring maximum flexibility.

Markets & Leverage


DNA Funded provides access to over 800 financial instruments, making it one of the most diverse prop trading firms for Indian traders.
  • Forex – Major, minor, and exotic currency pairs, including USD/INR (up to 1:50 leverage).
  • Commodities – Gold, silver, crude oil, and natural gas (up to 1:10 leverage).
  • Indices – Global indices such as NIFTY 50, US30, and GER40 (up to 1:10 leverage).
  • Stocks – US and European equities (up to 1:10 leverage).
  • Cryptocurrencies – Available for Single Helix and Double Helix challenges (up to 1:2 leverage).

Trading Platforms


DNA Funded supports two trading platforms, both with distinct advantages:
  • TradeLocker – A modern, web-based platform integrated with TradingView, featuring one-click execution, advanced charting tools, and a mobile-friendly interface. Recommended for manual traders and those who rely on TradingView indicators.
  • DXTrade – A simple yet functional platform suited for beginners, though some execution delays were noted during testing, making it less ideal for high-frequency traders.

Trust & Support


DNA Funded is a newly launched prop trading firm, but its partnership with DNA Markets—an ASIC-regulated forex broker—adds credibility to its offering. The prop firm has received positive early feedback for its transparent fee structure and strong trading conditions. It is also actively building a trader community through Discord, providing support, strategy discussions, and firm updates.

Once again, we checked industry sources to verify DNA Funded’s reputation, with PropFirms co-founder Noam Korbl stating: 

“DNA Funded has quickly built a reputation as one of the most well-rounded prop firms in the industry. With a strong foundation backed by a trusted forex broker, it offers traders a rare combination of reliability, affordability, and flexibility. Few firms manage to strike the right balance between challenge fairness, competitive payouts, and seamless execution—but DNA Funded delivers on all fronts.”

Final Verdict


DNA Funded is one of the most competitive prop trading firms in India, with low fees, direct broker backing, and flexible trading options. While it is still a new entrant to the market, its strong partnership with DNA Markets and access to over 800 instruments make it a great option for traders looking for low-cost evaluations and reliable execution.

Use promo code PROPFIRMS25 for an exclusive 25% discount on challenge fees.

4. BrightFunded – Best Prop Trader Rewards Program


BrightFunded is a top prop trading firm in India with flexible challenges, custom funding options, and a unique Trade2Earn loyalty program. Traders can earn perks like free challenges, higher profit splits, and better risk parameters. With unlimited scaling and no time limits on evaluations, it's a great choice for long-term account growth.

Use promo code PROPFIRMS20 for an exclusive 20% discount on challenge fees.


Evaluation Process


BrightFunded uses a three-phase evaluation model designed to test profitability, consistency, and risk management while allowing traders to proceed at their own pace.
  • Phase 1 – Evaluation – Traders must meet a profit target while maintaining moderate drawdown limits.
  • Phase 2 – Verification – The profit target is reduced, ensuring traders can sustain profitability under realistic conditions.
  • Phase 3 – Funded Account – Successful traders gain access to a funded demo account with profit splits up to 100%, plus unlimited scaling potential.

BrightFunded also offers challenge add-ons, allowing traders to reduce payout timeframes, increase profit splits, or remove minimum trading day requirements.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


Once funded, you start with an 80% profit split, which can increase to 90% or even 100% through the Trade2Earn program or challenge add-ons. BrightFunded processes withdrawals every 14 days by default, but traders can opt for a 7-day payout cycle for an additional fee. There are no minimum withdrawal thresholds, meaning you can withdraw profits without restrictions.

Markets & Leverage


BrightFunded provides access to a broad range of tradable assets with competitive leverage options:
  • Forex – Major, minor, and exotic currency pairs, including USD/INR (up to 1:100 leverage).
  • Commodities – Gold, silver, crude oil, and natural gas (up to 1:50 leverage).
  • Indices – Global indices such as NIFTY 50, S&P 500, and DAX 30 (up to 1:20 leverage).
  • Cryptocurrencies – Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, and other digital assets (up to 1:10 leverage).

Trading Platforms


BrightFunded operates on a proprietary trading platform, available on desktop, mobile, and web. The platform provides real-time market data and a user-friendly interface, making it accessible for all experience levels. However, it does not support MetaTrader 4 (MT4) or MetaTrader 5 (MT5), which may be a limitation for traders who rely on Expert Advisors (EAs) or broker-backed execution.

Trust & Support


BrightFunded has built a strong reputation for its fast payouts, transparent funding model, and active trader support. Customer service is available 24/7 via live chat and email, and the firm is highly engaged with traders through its Discord community and social media channels.

Final Verdict


BrightFunded is one of the best prop trading firms for Indian traders looking for customisation, scaling potential, and trading rewards. The flexible evaluation process, fast payouts, and Trade2Earn loyalty program add value for active traders. However, the lack of MT4/MT5 and the simulated trading environment may not suit those who prefer broker-backed firms with direct market access.

Use promo code PROPFIRMS20 for an exclusive 20% discount on challenge fees.

5. ThinkCapital – Low Fee Prop Challenges for Indian Traders


ThinkCapital is the fifth-best prop trading firm in India, offering low-cost evaluations, high-profit splits, and bi-weekly payouts. Backed by ThinkMarkets, a regulated global broker, it provides tight spreads, fast execution, and secure withdrawals. With account sizes up to $200,000 and profit splits reaching 90%, ThinkCapital is a strong choice for cost-conscious traders.

Evaluation Process


ThinkCapital offers three challenge types designed to suit different trading strategies and risk tolerances:
  • Lightning Challenge – A one-step evaluation with relaxed risk parameters, ideal for traders who want fast funding.
  • Dual Step Challenge – A structured two-step evaluation requiring disciplined risk management and steady performance.
  • Nexus Challenge – A multi-phase challenge designed for traders who want long-term account growth and scaling potential.

Each evaluation model includes customisation add-ons, such as faster payouts, increased profit splits, and news trading access.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


After passing the evaluation, you receive a funded account with an 80% profit split, which can be increased to 90% through an add-on. Withdrawals are processed every 14 days, with a weekly payout option available for an additional fee.

ThinkCapital’s scaling plan allows you to grow your account up to $1 million by maintaining consistent profitability over a set period.

Markets & Leverage


ThinkCapital provides access to a diverse range of trading instruments, with leverage levels varying based on the challenge type:
  • Forex – Major, minor, and exotic currency pairs, including USD/INR (up to 1:100 leverage).
  • Indices – Global stock indices, including NIFTY 50, NASDAQ 100, and DAX 40 (up to 1:15 leverage).
  • Commodities – Gold, silver, crude oil, and natural gas (up to 1:20 leverage on ThinkTrader, 1:10 on MT5).
  • Cryptocurrencies – Bitcoin, Ethereum, and other major crypto assets (up to 1:2 leverage on ThinkTrader, 1:1 on MT5).

Trading Platforms


ThinkCapital supports three major trading platforms, catering to different trading styles:
  • MetaTrader 5 (MT5) – Best for algorithmic traders with support for Expert Advisors (EAs) and advanced charting tools.
  • ThinkTrader + TradingView – A user-friendly platform with seamless TradingView integration, ideal for manual traders.

Trust & Support


ThinkCapital has a positive TrustPilot rating, with traders praising its clear evaluation rules, reliable payouts, and strong broker backing. The prop firm maintains an active Discord community, where traders can share insights, discuss strategies, and get updates.

Customer support is available via live chat and email, with response times generally quick during market hours. While ThinkCapital’s support is highly rated, some traders have noted that response times can slow during peak periods. The firm’s educational content includes a YouTube channel, covering platform tutorials, trading psychology, and market strategies.

Final Verdict


ThinkCapital is a cost-effective choice for Indian traders seeking low-fee challenges, high-profit splits, and bi-weekly payouts. Its broker backing by ThinkMarkets, structured evaluations, and access to MT5 and ThinkTrader make it a strong contender in the prop trading space. However, crypto-only payouts and a smaller community size may be drawbacks for some.

6. IC Funded – Top Forex Prop Firm


IC Funded is the sixth-best prop trading firm in India, offering structured evaluations, low-cost challenges, and broker-backed trading conditions. As an official partner of IC Markets, a globally regulated forex and CFD broker, IC Funded ensures tight spreads, deep liquidity, and fast execution.

For Indian traders, use promo code PROPFIRMS25 for 25% off your challenge fee, making IC Funded one of the most affordable forex prop firms.


Evaluation Process


IC Funded uses a two-step challenge model designed to assess both profitability and risk management.
  • Phase 1 – Evaluation – Traders must meet a moderate profit target while maintaining disciplined risk limits.
  • Phase 2 – Verification – Profit targets are reduced in this phase, ensuring traders can perform consistently under realistic market conditions.

Account sizes range from $5,000 to $500,000, with varying fees and risk parameters. The evaluation structure is straightforward, with clear scaling opportunities for traders who meet performance benchmarks.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


After passing the challenge, you receive a funded account with an initial 75% profit split, which increases to 80% after the first month. Withdrawals are available monthly, bi-weekly, or on-demand, giving you flexibility in accessing earnings.

IC Funded also refunds the challenge fee after your first profit withdrawal, making it an affordable choice for traders who successfully complete the evaluation.

Markets & Leverage


IC Funded focuses on forex and CFD trading, offering high leverage across key asset classes:
  • Forex – A broad selection of major, minor, and exotic currency pairs (up to 50:1 leverage).
  • Commodities – Gold, silver, crude oil, and other key commodities (up to 50:1 leverage).
  • Indices – Major global indices, including NIFTY 50, US30, and GER40 (up to 10:1 leverage).

Unlike some competitors, IC Funded does not offer stock or cryptocurrency trading.

Trading Platforms


IC Funded supports three major trading platforms, giving you access to advanced trading tools and automation features:
  • MetaTrader 4 (MT4) – A widely used platform with fast execution and Expert Advisor (EA) support.
  • MetaTrader 5 (MT5) – An upgraded version with more order types, market depth, and enhanced charting.
  • cTrader – Ideal for manual traders who prefer an intuitive interface and advanced risk management tools.

Trust & Support


IC Funded has a positive reputation among traders, with strong backing from IC Markets ensuring reliable execution and deep liquidity. The prop trading firm is well-regarded for its transparent challenge rules, low spreads, and fast withdrawals.

Customer support is available via live chat and email, with response times generally fast during market hours. While the prop firm does not have the largest trading community, its partnership with a well-established broker adds credibility.

Final Verdict


IC Funded is a top choice for forex traders looking for low fees, tight spreads, and a trusted broker partnership. The lack of stock and crypto trading may not suit all traders, but its strong forex focus, bi-weekly payouts, and discounted challenge fees with PROPFIRMS25 make it one of the best broker-backed prop firms available.

Use promo code PROPFIRMS25 to get 25% off your challenge fee and start trading with a trusted forex prop firm.

7. Blueberry Funded – Best Crypto Prop Firm for Indian Traders


Blueberry Funded is the seventh-best prop trading firm in India, offering low-cost challenges, flexible scaling, and cryptocurrency trading—a feature many prop firms lack. Broker-backed by Blueberry Markets, a regulated forex broker under ASIC, it provides traders with reliable execution, tight spreads, and a strong trading infrastructure.

For Indian traders, use promo code PROPFIRMS25 for 25% off your challenge fee, making Blueberry Funded one of the most affordable choices for crypto, forex, and commodities trading.


Evaluation Process


Blueberry Funded offers three evaluation models, allowing you to choose based on your trading style and risk preference.
  • 1 Step Challenge – A single-phase evaluation with a moderate profit target and a static drawdown. No time limits apply.
  • 2 Step Challenge – A structured two-step process with relaxed profit targets, designed to assess long-term consistency.
  • Rapid Challenge – A fast-track evaluation with a 7-day limit and a trailing drawdown, ideal for aggressive traders.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


Once funded, you receive an account between $5,000 and $200,000, with a scaling plan up to $2 million for high-performing traders. The profit split starts at 80% and can increase to 90%, depending on your performance.

Withdrawals are processed every 14 days through RiseWorks, ensuring a smooth payout experience.

Markets & Leverage


Blueberry Funded supports a wide range of markets, including forex, indices, commodities, and cryptocurrencies.
  • Forex – Major, minor, and exotic currency pairs (up to 1:50 leverage).
  • Indices – Key global indices, including NIFTY 50 and US30 (up to 1:10 leverage).
  • Commodities – Gold, crude oil, and silver (up to 1:10 leverage).
  • Cryptocurrencies – Bitcoin, Ethereum, and altcoins (up to 1:2 leverage).

Trading Platforms


You can trade on MT4, MT5, and DXTrade, offering a mix of manual, algorithmic, and web-based trading solutions.
  • MT4 – Classic forex trading platform with automated trading (EAs).
  • MT5 – Advanced charting, more order types, and broader asset coverage.
  • DXTrade – Browser-based platform with an intuitive interface, but fewer automation tools.

Trust & Support


Blueberry Funded has positive reviews for its low challenge fees, crypto access, and scaling options. However, it enforces strict news trading restrictions, which may not suit all traders.

Customer support is available 24/5 via live chat and email, with a Discord community offering additional trader support. While it lacks extensive educational resources, the firm is transparent about its trading conditions and payout processes.

Final Verdict


Blueberry Funded is one of the best prop firms for crypto traders in India, offering low fees, crypto access, and a strong broker partnership. The scaling plan to $2 million, bi-weekly payouts, and high profit splits make it a compelling option. However, its strict trading rules and limited payment options may not suit every trader.

Use promo code PROPFIRMS25 for 25% off your challenge fee and start trading with a cost-effective, crypto-friendly prop firm.

8. FTMO – Best Stock Prop Firm for Indian Traders


FTMO is the eighth-best proprietary trading firm in India, offering access to global stock markets, high-profit splits, and a structured evaluation process. Unlike broker-backed firms, FTMO operates independently but partners with liquidity providers to deliver institutional trading conditions.

For Indian traders, FTMO stands out due to its diverse asset selection, including USD/INR forex trading and major global stocks like Tesla (TSLA) and NIFTY 50 CFDs.

Evaluation Process


FTMO’s evaluation consists of a two-phase challenge, ensuring traders demonstrate profitability, risk management, and consistency.
  • Standard Challenge – A balanced account type with moderate profit targets and a 5% daily loss limit.
  • Aggressive Challenge – Higher profit targets with increased drawdown limits, ideal for risk-tolerant traders.
  • Swing Challenge – Designed for long-term traders, allowing weekend holding and news trading, but with reduced leverage.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


After passing the evaluation, you receive a funded account between $10,000 and $200,000, with scaling up to $2 million for top-performing traders.

Profit splits start at 80% and increase to 90% through FTMO’s scaling program. Payouts are available every 14 days with no withdrawal fees, and your challenge fee is refunded upon passing the evaluation.

Markets & Leverage


FTMO provides a broad range of asset classes, catering to forex, stock, and commodity traders.
  • Forex – Major, minor, and exotic currency pairs, including USD/INR (up to 1:100 leverage).
  • Stocks – Leading global equities such as Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), and NIFTY 50 CFDs.
  • Indices – Major global indices, including US30, GER40, and UK100.
  • Commodities – Gold, crude oil, and natural gas.
  • Cryptocurrencies – Bitcoin, Ethereum, and select crypto pairs.
  • Bonds – Government bonds for diversification.

Trading Platforms


FTMO supports MT4, MT5, cTrader, and DXTrade, offering flexible access on desktop, web, and mobile.
  • MT4 & MT5 – Industry-standard platforms for forex and stock trading with automated strategies.
  • cTrader – A modern alternative with a clean interface and advanced order execution.
  • DXTrade – A web-based trading platform designed for seamless market access.

Trust & Support


FTMO has built a strong reputation for its structured evaluation, fast payouts, and scaling opportunities. It offers 24/5 customer support via live chat and email, but traders should note news trading restrictions and weekend trade limitations on some accounts.

The prop trading firm also maintains an active trader community, with educational resources, market insights, and trader interviews.

Final Verdict


FTMO is one of the best prop firms for Indian traders interested in stock and forex trading, offering USD/INR forex access, high-profit splits, and a structured scaling plan. The ability to trade major global stocks makes it an excellent choice for equity traders. However, traders should consider the two-phase challenge structure and challenge fees before committing.

9. OANDA Prop Trader – Prop Firm for Beginners


OANDA Prop Trader is a trusted, broker-backed proprietary trading firm offering funded accounts up to $500,000 through a structured two-step evaluation. Backed by OANDA, a globally regulated forex and CFD broker, it provides raw spreads from 0.0 pips, a commission-based pricing model, and a trailing or static drawdown system depending on the challenge type.

Use promo code PROPFIRMS15 for 15% off all challenges (excluding 500K accounts).


Evaluation Process


OANDA offers two evaluation models, Classic Challenge and Boost Challenge, catering to different risk preferences and payout structures.
  • Classic Challenge – A beginner-friendly option with 80% profit splits, a trailing drawdown, and account sizes up to $500,000.
  • Boost Challenge – Designed for experienced traders, offering 90% profit splits after the first withdrawal and a static drawdown system.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


OANDA provides funded accounts ranging from $5,000 to $500,000, allowing traders to scale up without risking personal capital. The profit split starts at 80% and increases to 90% for Boost Challenge traders. Payouts are processed every 14 days, with a minimum withdrawal requirement of $200.

Markets & Leverage


OANDA Prop Trader offers a selection of tradable assets, though it does not support cryptocurrency markets.
  • Forex – 36 major and minor currency pairs with up to 100:1 leverage.
  • Indices – 15 global indices, including US30 and JP225, with up to 50:1 leverage.
  • Commodities – Gold, silver, and crude oil with 50:1 leverage.

Trading Platforms


OANDA Prop Trader exclusively supports MetaTrader 5 (MT5) across desktop, web, and mobile devices. While MT5 is a strong platform for forex and CFD trading, traders who prefer TradingView or cTrader may find the lack of options restrictive.

Trust & Support


As a broker-backed prop trading firm, OANDA benefits from strong regulatory oversight and positive industry recognition. The evaluation process is fair, payouts are reliable, and customer support is available 24/5. However, higher spreads on certain assets, platform limitations, and a high withdrawal minimum could make other prop firms more appealing to traders seeking tighter conditions.

Final Verdict 


OANDA Prop Trader offers a secure, broker-backed opportunity with low challenge fees, fair profit splits, and a simple evaluation process. While it has a strong forex and commodities focus, traders looking for crypto trading or platform variety may prefer other firms.

Use promo code PROPFIRMS15 for 15% off all challenges (excluding 500K accounts).

10. The Funded Trader – Largest Prop Trading Community


The Funded Trader is one of the largest proprietary trading firms, offering multiple evaluation models, flexible scaling up to $5 million, and a highly active trader community. Unlike broker-backed firms, The Funded Trader operates independently, using a simulated funding model. While it provides diverse challenge types and high profit splits, its complex rules, inconsistent support, and higher trading costs may be drawbacks for some traders.

Evaluation Process


The Funded Trader offers five challenge types, allowing traders to choose based on their risk tolerance and trading strategy.
  • Knight Challenge (One Step) – A single-phase challenge with a 3% daily drawdown and 6% total drawdown, designed for traders seeking a simple evaluation.
  • Knight Pro Challenge (One Step) – A more flexible challenge with a 5% daily drawdown and 8% total drawdown, but fewer add-ons for customization.
  • Royal Challenge (Two Step) – A two-phase challenge requiring an 8% profit target in Phase 1 and 5% in Phase 2, ideal for traders who prefer a structured evaluation.
  • Royal Pro Challenge (Two Step) – Offers higher profit splits and faster payouts (first payout after 3 days) but requires additional fees for certain add-ons.
  • Dragon Challenge (Three Step) – A strict three-phase evaluation with progressive profit targets and risk management requirements, suited for experienced traders.

Funded Accounts & Profit Splits


Once funded, you can trade account sizes ranging from $5,000 to $200,000, with the potential to scale up to $5 million if you maintain a 6% profit over three months. Profit splits start at 75% and go up to 95%, depending on the challenge type and add-ons.

Payouts can be requested as early as 3 days for certain accounts, but others have withdrawal cycles of 14 or 30 days. A key drawback for high-volume forex traders is the commission fee of $4 per lot, which is above industry averages.

Markets & Leverage


The Funded Trader provides access to a wide range of instruments, though leverage varies across account types.
  • Forex – 1:200 leverage during challenges, reduced to 1:100 in funded accounts.
  • Indices – 1:20 leverage for most challenges, lowered to 1:5 in the Knight Challenge.
  • Commodities & Metals – 1:30 leverage, covering assets like gold and silver.
  • Crypto – 1:2 leverage in challenges, reduced to 1:1 in funded accounts, limiting crypto trading flexibility.

Trading Platforms


The Funded Trader supports MT5, cTrader, Match-Trader, and DXTrade. However, traders who prefer cTrader must pay a $25 add-on fee, and DXTrade has reported execution issues, which could impact reliability.

Trust & Support


The Funded Trader has a large community, offering active Discord and social media engagement. However, customer support response times are inconsistent, and rule enforcement has been a concern for some traders. Its lack of broker backing may also be a factor for those looking for more security.

Final Verdict


The Funded Trader is a solid choice for traders looking for diverse challenge types, high scaling potential, and an engaged trading community. However, its complex rules, high commissions, and inconsistent customer support may make it less appealing for those seeking lower costs and a smoother experience. If transparency and trading costs are priorities, other prop firms may be a better fit.

FAQs


Which trading prop firm is the best?


The best proprietary trading firm in India is FundedNext, offering high-profit splits, multiple challenge types, and a scaling plan up to $4 million. FXIFY is another strong choice with instant funding options and flexible evaluations, while DNA Funded provides tight spreads and broker-backed liquidity. Each firm has unique benefits, so the best option depends on your trading style and risk tolerance.

Which funded account is best in India?


The best-funded account in India depends on your trading goals. FundedNext offers accounts up to $200,000 with a scaling plan to $4 million, making it ideal for traders looking for long-term growth. BrightFunded also offers one of the best funded accounts with itsTrade2Earn rewards program, allowing traders to earn perks like free challenges and increased profit splits. Both prop trading firms provide flexible evaluation models, fast payouts, and strong funding opportunities for Indian traders.

What is the most trusted prop firm?


The most trusted prop firms are those with a strong industry reputation, transparent rules, and fast payouts. FundedNext, FXIFY, and DNA Funded are highly rated, with positive trader reviews and reliable withdrawal processes. Broker-backed firms like OANDA Prop Trader and IC Funded provide additional security by operating under regulated brokerage firms. You can view our full evaluation done by Noam Korbl at PropFirms.
"Moneycontrol journalists were not involved in the creation of the article. The views expressed here do not represent the stand of this publication."

data.title: FII net sell shares worth Rs 1,628 crore, DIIs net buyers of Rs 1,510 crore
data.description:
FIIs have sold Indian equities worth Rs 1.63 lakh crore in 2025 thus far.

Foreign institutional investors (FII/FPI) were net sellers on March 12, offloading shares worth Rs 1,627.61 crore while domestic institutional investors (DII) were net buyers of shares worth Rs 1,510.35 crore, provisional data showed.

During the trading session of March 12, FIIs net bought shares worth Rs 13,088.94 crore and sold shares worth Rs 14,716.55 crore. DIIs purchased shares worth Rs 11,803.42 crore and sold shares worth Rs 10,293.07 crore.

For the year so far, FIIs have been net sellers of shares worth Rs 1.63 lakh crore, while DIIs have net bought Rs 1.76 lakh crore worth of shares.

Indian benchmarks, the Nifty 50 and Sensex, recovered from intraday lows to end March 12’s volatile session near the flatline. Early gains faded amid heavy IT stock selling, but strength in Bajaj Finance, HDFC Bank, ITC, Tata Motors, and Reliance Industries helped pare losses.

Caution prevailed ahead of the US inflation report, as concerns grew that Trump's tariff hikes could drive prices higher, forcing the Federal Reserve to keep rates elevated. This stoked fears of a US recession and a broader global trade war, with the EU planning retaliatory tariffs on 28.33 billion dollars worth of US goods.

Weak global cues added to market jitters, with Wall Street extending losses and Asian equities under pressure. "Uncertainties around global trade and recession fears continue to weigh on sentiment," said Vinod Nair, Head of Research at Geojit Financial Services.

At close, the Sensex fell 72.56 points or 0.10 percent to 74,029.76, while the Nifty slipped 27.40 points or 0.12 percent to 22,470.50. Market breadth remained weak, with 1,440 shares advancing, 2,383 declining, and 129 unchanged.

On the technical front, Jatin Gedia of Mirae Asset Sharekhan noted that the Nifty held the 22,300 support level despite IT sector pressure. With a positive crossover on daily and hourly momentum indicators, he maintained a bullish short-term outlook, targeting 22,800 to 23,000, with key support at 22,300 to 22,250.

data.title: Wall Street indices rebound after soft inflation print; tech stocks soar
data.description:
Tech stocks led the rally on Wall Street.

Wall Street indices retreated from losses on March 12 after a softer-than-expected inflation print for February bolstered investor sentiment, sparking a rally across tech stocks. With the rebound, the S&P 500 bounced back from a three-week rout, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite recovered from steep fall seen earlier this week.

The S&P 500 rose 0.8 percent, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 1.6 percent. The Dow Jones Industrial Average edged up 0.1 percent. AI giant Nvidia surged over 6 percent, while Meta Platforms and Tesla climbed 4 percent and 8 percent, respectively.

The US consumer price index rose 0.2 percent for the February, bringing annual inflation to 2.8 percent—both below Dow Jones estimates of 0.3 percent and 2.9 percent. Core CPI, excluding food and energy, rose 0.2 percent on month and 3.1 percent annually, also below expectations.

It was concerns over Trump's trade policies triggered a spike in inflation and sparking growth slowdown in the US had contributed to the recent sell-off. However, the CPI report eased some of those fears and left room for the Federal Reserve to consider rate cuts if need be.

That aside, Trump's trade policies have remained an overhang on global markets, particularly the US, with his recent wave of additional tariffs on Canadian steel and aluminium imports further intensifying fears of disruption to the global trade setup.

Meanwhile, in response to Trump's tariffs, the European Union announced plans to impose counter-tariffs on $28.33 billion worth of US imports starting in April, adding to market jitters.

This week alone, the Dow and S&P 500 have each dropped over 3 percent, while the Nasdaq has fallen 4 percent. The S&P 500 briefly entered correction territory on Tuesday, down 10 percent from its February peak. Over the past month, the S&P 500 has lost nearly 8 percent, with the Dow and Nasdaq declining 6.6 percent and 11.3 percent, respectively.

data.title: Consumers don't buy commodities, they put a lot of trust on brand and quality: Ather CEO on IPO confidence
data.description:
Tarun Mehta, Cofounder and CEO of Ather Energy

Ather Energy CEO Tarun Mehta dismissed concerns over the company’s valuation cut ahead of its planned IPO, emphasising that customers buy its electric two-wheelers for quality and service rather than as a 'commodity.'

His remarks come a day after reports suggest that Ather is considering lowering its listing valuation to $1.2 billion–$1.6 billion from an earlier $2 billion target while looking to raise $400 million.

“I think the job of the company is not to try and time the market. We've got a good business; we are hopefully scaling it up well. Beyond that, markets obviously always decide. But I am not a big fan of trying to time too much. Other industry peers, competitors, have multiple approaches and I think our approach is quite unique,” Mehta told Moneycontrol during a media interaction on the side lines of TiE Con Mumbai 2025 on March 12.

Mehta said he is confident of his “product, tech and quality-focused brand”.

“We believe that is central to building a long-term enduring auto company in India. Consumers don't buy commodities here. Consumers put a lot of trust in the auto brand they are buying. There is an enormous power of word of mouth and all that comes down to the quality a product has,” he said.

He added, “The reliability is seen and how good, how happy the customers are. So, between the twin intersections of the product experience, which is where the magic lies, and quality, which is what will hopefully ensure good word of mouth, there always has been a focus.”

He emphasised on the Ather stack strategy of building the entire ecosystem of hardware, product, largest charging network and software for good customer experience. Ather scooters have features such as auto hold, magic twist, to name a few, he said.

Overall, the Indian electric two-wheeler market has seen significant shifts in recent months, with traditional manufacturers Bajaj Auto and TVS Motor making notable gains in market share. In December 2024, Bajaj Auto led the market with a nearly 25% share, selling 18,276 units, while TVS Motor secured a 23.48% share with 17,212 units sold.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Listed rival, Ola Electric, previously the market leader, saw its share decline to 18.78% during the same period, with sales of 13,769 units.

Open-sourcing charging network

Ather is currently focused on building the largest network for open-source fast charging. Mehta shared that Ather has about 3,000-3,500 fast chargers on its network across the country.
It had doubled from 1,000-1500 fast chargers until last year, the company plans to announce its next expansion in a few months.

“For us, the first priority still is a widespread network of fast and ultra-fast chargers with very high uptimes. And towards that, we need to try more industry collaboration which is why we open sourced a standard. The Ather standard is not even called the Ather standard, it’s called LEX, Light Electric Combined Charging System, and BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards) has accepted it now,” Mehta said.

He added that multiple OEMs have now adopted this standard, and Ather doesn’t plan to charge any installation or onboarding fee for the same.

“It's stupid for us to say, no, we will sell our charging standard. We'll put a royalty curtain behind it. No one will pay for it. Everybody will set up 10 different charging infrastructures. So, I think it's far better to just dissolve those barriers and make it easy for people to stop wasting time,” he said.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Net inflow into equity funds hit year low in February, SIPs dip to three-month low
data.description:
For the month, inflows through systematic investment plan (SIP) fell to a three-month low of Rs 25,999 crore, according to AMFI.


While net inflows into equity funds remained in positive territory for the 48th month in a row, the net equity inflow for February fell by 26 percent on a month on month basis, according to data released by AMFI.

Overall, AMFI data showed the industry AUM for February fell to Rs 64 lakh crore from Rs 67 lakh crore a month ago. This decline was due to mark-to-market losses in equity funds, AMFI said. February also saw 29 NFO schemes launched across categories raising a total of Rs 4,029 crore.

Here are some of the key highlights from an equity flow perspective:

Inflows into equity funds see a dip
While the month-on-month numbers have seen a dip, most market experts remain positive on equity funds as an investment option. For the month, inflows were around Rs 29,303.34 crore, down from Rs 39,688 crore a month ago.

Morningstar's Nehal Meshram said investors are adopting a cautious yet steady approach, reassessing portfolios as they maintain long-term investment commitment. “Equity fund flows remained consistent across all categories, underscoring the broad appeal of equity markets among investors," she said.

Ventura Securities' Juzer Gabajiwala said retail investors may just be turning slightly cautious, and not panicking. "While small and midcap inflows have slowed recently, the drop in gross collections isn't drastic. Given the market narrative that mid and small caps are overvalued, investors are likely holding back and leaning more toward large-cap investments," he said.

Slowdown across equity categoriesThere were no outflows in the equity categories, but funds across categories saw a dip in net flows when compared to the previous month -- except focussed fund category, that saw an increase of around 65 percent. The highest flows in the space was in thematic funds with inflows of around Rs 5,711.58 crore. Flexi-cap funds saw the second-highest net inflows of Rs 5,104.22 crore followed by small-cap and mid-cap funds at Rs 3,722.46 crore and Rs 3,406.95 crore, respectively.

Abhishek Tiwari, Executive Director & Chief Business Officer, PGIM India Mutual Fund said market volatility could continue for some more time and is advising investors to say invested, and avoid timing the market.

SIP continues to find investor interest
For February, inflows through systematic investment plan (SIP) route fell to a three-month low of Rs 25,999 crore versus Rs 26,400 crore a month ago. The SIP AUM fell to Rs 12.38 lakh crore from Rs 13.19 lakh crore in the previous month.  SIP stoppage was around 123% in February, up from 109 percent in January 2025.

New tax rules impacting ELSS flows
Mutual fund experts believe a surprising trend this month has been the slowdown in flows towards ELSS funds that usually see higher flows during the months of January and February. They attribute the slowdown to provisions of the new tax regime announced in the Union Budget. For the month, flows into ELSS funds was down 22 percent to Rs 614 crore from Rs 797 crore in the previous month.

data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Daily Voice: Ben Powell of BlackRock rules out potential US recession risk, expects India's economic outperformance to continue
data.description:
Ben Powell is the Chief Middle East and APAC Investment Strategist at the BlackRock Investment Institute

Markets have been increasingly pricing in a potential US recession. But "we disagree," said Ben Powell of BlackRock Investment Institute in an interview to Moneycontrol.

In the US, "job creation has slowed slightly but the labour market remains strong in contrast to soft survey data showing declining consumer confidence. US corporate earnings are also holding up," he reasoned.

The Chief Middle East and APAC Investment Strategist at BlackRock Investment Institute believes India stands out as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally.

The International Monetary Fund (IMF) projects a 6.5 percent GDP (gross domestic product) growth for India in 2025 — well above the global and emerging market averages. Powell expects this economic outperformance to continue, driven by structural strengths, such as favourable demographics and rising productivity.

He is of the view that this broader structural story presents opportunities.

Are you concerned about global growth due to the trade war?

Our scenarios framework – mapping potential outcomes for different mixes of growth, inflation and policy responses – helps us navigate this evolving market and economic landscape. In the past few weeks, the markets have been increasingly pricing in a potential US recession. We disagree. Why? Job creation has slowed slightly but the labour market remains strong in contrast to soft survey data showing declining consumer confidence. US corporate earnings are also holding up. We still think earnings strength can broaden out beyond tech and to other regions as the buildout and adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) progresses. While heightened policy uncertainty will drive near-term market volatility, other drivers keep us overweight on US stocks.

Do you expect India to sustain more than 6 percent growth in the coming years?

India stands out as one of the fastest-growing major economies, globally. The IMF projects a GDP growth of 6.5 percent in 2025 — well above global and emerging market averages. We expect this economic outperformance to persist, driven by structural strengths such as favourable demographics and rising productivity. This broader structural story presents opportunities, in our view.

India’s demographic edge is a cornerstone of our upbeat long-term outlook. A young and expanding workforce is set to sustain consumption growth and drive productivity gains over the coming decades. This demographic dividend, combined with the possibility of AI-driven productivity gains, could amplify India’s economic potential, in our view.

India’s structural strengths outweigh near-term softness from weak domestic sentiments and uncertainty over US trade policy, in our view. A narrow focus on cyclical trends — such as slowing growth — risks missing the bigger picture and the opportunities it create.

Signs of moderating growth and easing inflation have prompted the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to act. The central bank recently cut its key interest rate for the first time in nearly five years, lowering the repo rate from 6.5 percent to 6.25 percent to help counter slowing growth. We expect further rate cuts over the next five years.

What more would you like to see from the government in terms of policies that can not only lift economic growth but also attract FDI on a large scale?

We see trade protectionism emerging as a key risk under the new US administration. For India, however, the impact of potential US tariffs look relatively contained — goods exports to the US make up less than 5 percent of its GDP, according to World Bank data. This suggests India is far less exposed to direct trade disruptions than economies more dependent on US demand.

That said, India could face disproportionate pressure due to the wide tariff gap between the two nations. India imposes higher tariffs on US imports than the US does on Indian goods. This imbalance has historically resulted in US businesses paying more in tariffs to India than vice versa, making India a potential target for policy retaliation.

The US and India agreed to launch bilateral trade talks for the first time. The outcome remains unclear — whether a formal treaty or a mix of tariff and regulatory agreements. Even if retaliatory tariffs were imposed, we believe the economic impact would remain limited.

Which sectors would you consider for long-term investment in India?

We advocate above-benchmark allocations to Indian equities within strategic portfolios with investment horizons of five years or more as the country’s economic transformation unfolds.

We believe structural shifts underway in India map neatly onto the five mega forces we see shaping the world now and in the future. These shifts include its demographic advantage, supercharged digitisation and resilience in navigating a fragmented geopolitical landscape.

India’s turning investment and capex cycle, along with recovering rural consumption, underpin long-term structural positives. The capex cycle is driven by strong corporate and government balance sheets and a robust pipeline of projects in utilities, renewables, manufacturing, and real estate.

Do you think the US equity markets are overvalued now?

Economies around the globe are being transformed by mega forces. These forces could continue to shift the long-term trend, making various outcomes possible. We think investors should keep the big picture in mind and can find opportunities by tapping into the waves of transformation we see ahead in the real economy. AI and the low-carbon transition require investment potentially on par with the industrial revolution.

As we rethink investing in our outlook, we believe that valuations are not that stretched, as we look beyond current valuations in this transformation. Valuations tend to matter more for returns over a long-term horizon than in the near term. Some US equity valuation measures – whether price-to-earnings ratios or equity risk premiums – look rich relative to history. But they may not tell the full story. The equity market’s changing sectoral composition reflects the transformation taking hold. So, comparing today's index to that of the past is like comparing apples to oranges.

Do you foresee the next rate cut from the US Federal Reserve only in the second half of 2025?

Inflation cooled across developed markets in 2024, but structural forces did the hard work, rather than central bank rate hikes -- notably, the post-pandemic normalisation of the goods and labour markets, and an unexpected rise in US immigration that expanded the labour force.

Last week’s US jobs report showed the pace of job creation has eased somewhat, but if immigration slows back towards historical levels – and we some evidence it is already slowing – job creation would need to slow quite a bit further to prevent inflation picking up. Indeed, wage pressures are still too high for inflation to settle back at the Fed’s 2 percent target.

We think persistent inflation pressures mean that the Fed will need to keep rates high for longer, with no more than one or two cuts this year. We see the Fed’s policy changes as a recalibration of policy back towards a neutral level, rather than a traditional ‘rate cutting cycle’ as part of a business cycle.

Do you believe the Trump administration's tariffs risk will persist for a couple of months more before easing?

The US briefly rolled out the largest tariffs in nearly a century on March 4 -- 25 percent tariffs on most Canadian and Mexican imports and an extra 10 percent on China. While most North American tariffs were later put on ice for another month, we think an average effective tariff rate of about 10 percent could be the eventual landing zone – with volatility along the way.

What matters more for near-term growth are pains due to elevated uncertainty, including a potential US government shutdown. The markets expect weaker US growth to push the Fed to cut policy rates, as in a typical business cycle. Yet, we see a tough trade-off between supporting growth and curbing sticky inflation, limiting how much the Fed can cut. That reinforces our expectation of policy rates above the pre-pandemic levels and higher bond yields.

data.title: Top gainers and losers today: Stocks that moved the most on March 12
data.description:
Top gainers and losers today: Stocks that moved the most on March 12

Domestic stock markets remained volatile on March 12, closing flat as global trade uncertainties and US recession fears dampened sentiment. The Sensex dipped 72.56 points (-0.10%) to 74,029.76, while the Nifty slipped 27.40 points (-0.12%) to 22,470.50.

Broader markets struggled, with BSE Midcap and Smallcap indices dropping 0.5%. Auto, banking, and pharma stocks provided some support, each rising 0.5%, but metal, IT, realty, telecom, PSU banks, and media sectors saw declines of 0.5-3%. While valuations have normalized to 5-year averages, market sentiment remains cautious as investors navigate economic uncertainty and await stronger domestic triggers.

Top Gainers

IndusInd Bank

After a brutal 27% crash in the previous session, IndusInd Bank saw a six percent rebound as investors assessed the fallout from the bank’s derivatives portfolio discrepancies. While concerns remain, bargain hunters and short-covering drove the stock higher. The bank had been under pressure following the revelation of potential mismatches in its trading book, but the recovery suggests that investors see limited long-term damage.

Banking Sector

Despite weakness in broader indices, banking stocks rallied in the range of a percent, helping Nifty Bank recover after a two-day losing streak. The buying interest in financial stocks signals some resilience in the sector, even as global macroeconomic conditions remain uncertain. Investors seem to be focusing on strong credit growth and stable asset quality trends in leading private and PSU banks.

Ola Electric

Ola Electric shares rose 2% after the company announced the successful completion of its company-wide cost-reduction initiative, titled the ‘Network Transformation and Opex Reduction Programme.’ The initiative is aimed at streamlining operations and optimizing costs, which the company believes will help its automotive segment achieve EBITDA breakeven by Q1 FY26.

Tata Motors

Shares of Tata Motors surged over 3% on March 12, emerging as one of the top Sensex gainers and providing a strong boost to the BSE Auto index. The rally was fueled by a series of positive brokerage reports, which reinforced investor confidence in the company's growth trajectory.

Top Losers 
IT Stocks

The Nifty IT index plunged nearly 2%, marking its lowest level since July 2024 and extending its 16% YTD decline. Infosys and Wipro led the sell-off amid growing fears that a slowdown in US client spending could impact revenue growth for Indian IT firms. Global investment bank Morgan Stanley issued a bearish note, citing risks from a shifting macroeconomic environment and technological disruptions, adding pressure on valuations.

Gensol Engineering

Gensol Engineering continued its downward spiral, declining 5% throughout the trading session. Since mid-February 2024, the stock has plummeted over 40%, erasing gains for late-stage investors who had entered during the rally. The steep fall follows a series of credit downgrades linked to concerns over the company's financial health.

PB Fintech

PB Fintech, the parent company of Policybazaar, saw its stock tumble over 5% after announcing a Rs 696 crore investment in its subsidiary PB Healthcare Services. While the move is aimed at expanding the company’s healthcare-related business, investors were not convinced about its immediate impact on profitability. The market reaction suggests that investors may be concerned about the capital allocation strategy and the timeline for returns on investment.

Capital Market Firms

Stocks of capital market firms came under pressure after data revealed that equity mutual fund inflows dropped 26% in February, reflecting increased investor caution amid market volatility. HDFC AMC, CAMS, and Angel One were among the worst affected, as lower inflows could impact future revenue growth for asset management and brokerage firms.

Kaynes Tech

Shares of Kaynes Technology witnessed a sharp 8% decline in early trade on March 12, following news that the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) issued a show-cause notice to the company’s Managing Director, Ramesh Kunhikannan, over insider trading allegations. However, the stock managed to recover some losses later in the session as investors assessed the potential impact of the regulatory action.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Slower-than-expected inflation opens door wider to April rate cut
data.description:
Economists said that the central bank may infuse another Rs 2 lakh crore in the next financial year, which could be essential for the transmission of the rate cut

Lower-than-expected consumer prices index (CPI) inflation, which was also below the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) medium-term target of 4 percent, has intensified the chances of another rate cut by the central bank in April monetary policy, economists said.

Additionally, economists believe that there could be some liquidity announcements by the central bank in the upcoming monetary policy on top of the measures announced recently to boost liquidity, considering the tightness in the liquidity.

"We see space for further monetary policy easing by RBI. We expect 25 basis points (Bps) cut in April and 25 bps cut in June," said Gaura Sengupta, Economist at IDFC First Bank.

Adding to this, Madhavi Arora, lead economist & Emkay Global Financial Services said despite the shocker on core, the pleasant headline CPI surprise implies that the 4QFY25 inflation may undershoot RBI’s forecast by more than 40 bps, and could keep the rate cut possibility in April solid, which was also reflected in the recent dovish policy minutes.

The central bank in February's monetary policy had cut repo rate by 25 basis points (bps) to 6.25 percent, while the rates for the Standing Deposit Facility, Marginal Standing Facility, and bank rates remained at 6.5 percent.

The RBI has increased the repo rate by 250 basis points between May 2022 and February 2023. Since April 2023, the repo rate has been steady at 6.5 percent, in order to keep a check on the inflation to bring it to the medium-term target of 4 percent.

India's retail inflation eased to a seven-month low of 3.61 percent in February, as food inflation eased further, according to data released by the government on March 12.

The decline in February marks only the third time this fiscal year that inflation has dipped below 4 percent.

The inflation number was below the MC poll median of 19 economists that pegged inflation at 3.8 percent.

Food inflation eased further below 6 percent for the first time since September 2024. India's food inflation had reduced below 8 percent for the first time in four months in January, as food prices had recorded 6 percent growth over the previous year.

The members of the monetary policy committee (MPC) in the February minutes also said concerns over India's economic growth and easing inflation trajectory made a room for the central bank to cut rates, showed February policy minutes.

"There has been a shift in the domestic growth inflation balance since the December 2024 policy – while the inflation registered sequential softening, growth outcomes were weaker. Heightening uncertainties, emanating from the global financial markets and trade policies too cloud the outlook for domestic growth and inflation," said RBI deputy governor M Rajeshwar Rao.

Further, RBI governor Sanjay Malhotra said that given the macroeconomic outlook when inflation is expected to align with the target, and recognising that monetary policy is forward-looking, he views a lower policy rate to be more appropriate at the current juncture.

On the liquidity front, economists said that the central bank may infuse another Rs 2 lakh crore in the next financial year, which could be essential for the transmission of the rate cut.

"We expect further liquidity infusion of inr2tn in FY26 to insure system liquidity is a mild positive. This is essential for transmission of rate cuts," Sengupta said.

Liquidity in the banking system has also been under stress in the last few months due to slower government spending, interventions in the foreign exchange market by the RBI, and heavy selling by foreign portfolio investors in Indian equities.

To support liquidity in the banking system, the RBI has since late 2024 infused around Rs 3 lakh crore worth of durable liquidity, tapping a combination of VRR or variable rate repo auctions, swaps and OMOs.

The board of directors of the Power Finance Corporation approved Rs 1,40,000 crore borrowing plan for 2025-26. The board in its meeting held on March 12, 2025, approved a total borrowing of Rs 1,40,000 crore for 2025-26, a regulatory filing stated.

The board of the state-owned company also approved declaration of fourth interim dividend at the rate of Rs 3.50 per equity share on the face value of the paid-up equity shares of Rs 10 each for 2024-25.

The company has also fixed March 19, 2025 as the record date for payment of dividend, while the payment of dividend will be on or before April 11, 2025.

Shares of Power Finance Corporation Ltd settled in red in Wednesday's trading session at Rs 396 per share, down 0.85 percent.

data.title: Technical View: Nifty end flat amid high volatility; support at 22,300-22,250
data.description:
Market Today

In yet another highly volatile session on March 12, the Nifty50 index managed to close with little change below 22,500.

The index opened higher ignoring weak global cues but soon erased all the gains to turn negative and witnessed extended selling as the day progress, but buying at lower levels helped to close at 22,470.50, down 27.40 points or 0.12 percent.

Infosys, Wipro, Tech Mahindra, Nestle, TCS were among top losers on the Nifty, while gainers were IndusInd Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Tata Motors, ITC, Bajaj Finance.

Nifty Midcap index shed 0.5 percent, erasing all the previous session gains. On the other hand smallcap index was down 0.2 percent, extending the losing streak on third consecutive session.

On the sectoral front, auto, private bank, oil & gas, pharma, energy were up 0.2-0.7 percent, while metal, IT, realty, PSU Bank, media were down 0.5-3 percent.

"The Nifty witnessed gap up opening and witnessed selling pressure during the first half. A smart recovery from the support zone helped the index to recover and close off the intraday lows though in the negative down 27 points," said Jatin Gedia – Technical Research Analyst at Mirae Asset Sharekhan.

"On the daily charts we can observe that the Nifty has again tested the support zone of 22300 and defended it successfully. Despite the pressure from the Nifty IT index, the selling pressure was absorbed and the Nifty closed only marginally in the negative."

"Daily and hourly momentum indicator has a positive crossover which is a buy signal. Thus, we shall continue to maintain our positive stance on the index for short term target of 22800-23000. Crucial support zone 22300 – 22250," he added.

After a positive start, the Bank Nifty index extended gains as the day progress to close at 48,056.65, up 0.42 percent, extending the gain on third straight session.

"Bank Nifty followed through on yesterday’s sweep formation, closing above the previous day’s high after four consecutive down candles. This confirms bullish momentum, but a sustained move above 48,200 is key to triggering fresh buying and short covering," said Anshul Jain, Head of Research, Lakshmishree Investments.

"If the index holds above this level, it can swiftly target 48,800. On the downside, dips toward the 48,000–47,900 zone present buying opportunities, provided there is bullish confirmation. Traders should watch for strength above 48,200 for continuation, while protecting long positions if the index fails to hold key support levels, analyst said.
